claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children. Let me begin with a basic question:

**How many children are you inquiring about, and what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - his name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to understand your circumstances better. Let me start with this question:

**How old is Alex, and what is his date of birth?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Before I ask further questions, let me check the official eligibility rules to ensure I'm assessing your situation correctly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming Alex's age. I can see that at 17 years old, there are specific rules that apply. According to the eligibility guidelines, if a child is 16 or over, you can normally still qualify for Child Benefit if they're under 20 and staying in approved education or training.

To help determine if you're eligible for Alex, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he at school, college, or in an apprenticeship?)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. According to the eligibility rules, if a child starts an apprenticeship in England, Child Benefit stops. However, I need to clarify one thing to make sure I have the complete picture:

**Is Alex's apprenticeship in England, or is it in another part of the UK (Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's in England. He's training to be an electrician.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Result: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

The reason is that Alex has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, while you can normally continue to claim Child Benefit for children aged 16-19 who stay in approved education or training, there is a specific rule that stops your eligibility if your child starts an apprenticeship in England.

Since Alex has recently started his electrician apprenticeship in England, you will not be eligible for Child Benefit for him under the current rules.

If you have any other children you'd like me to assess, or if you have further questions about this decision, please let me know.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the eligibility rules, while Child Benefit can normally continue for children aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training, there is a specific rule stating that you will stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Since Alex has recently started an apprenticeship as an electrician in England, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning:
You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because he has started an apprenticeship in England, which triggers the cessation of eligibility under the Child Benefit rules."/>
</invoke>

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the eligibility rules, while Child Benefit can normally continue for children aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training, there is a specific rule stating that you will stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. Since Alex has recently started an apprenticeship as an electrician in England, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for him.

response.overall_reasoning:
You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because he has started an apprenticeship in England, which triggers the cessation of eligibility under the Child Benefit rules."/>
</invoke>
